overview.Title: Water Treatment Plant Sabotage Through Complex Cyber Attack
overview.Summary: A sophisticated cyber attack targeting a water treatment plant, involving multiple stages including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

threat_actor_profile.Motivation: Motivation: Likely nation-state sponsored, targeting critical infrastructure for potential sabotage or industrial espionage.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather OSINT to identify exposed SCADA systems and outdated software, using web scraping tools to collect information from public sources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 673 - Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software
stage.description: The attackers develop specialized malware targeting known vulnerabilities in SCADA systems, potentially compromising the software development lifecycle.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Using phishing emails to compromise employee credentials and gain initial access to the target network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 - Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation
stage.description: Deploying custom malware to infiltrate the target network undetected, leveraging known vulnerabilities in SCADA systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 673 - Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software
stage.description: Establishing backdoors for sustained access to critical servers, ensuring persistent presence in the target network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Exploiting known vulnerabilities to escalate privileges within the SCADA system, gaining control over critical processes.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 608 - Cryptanalysis of Cellular Encryption
stage.description: Encrypting communications to evade network monitoring and detection, ensuring stealthy operations within the network.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 - Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Stealing credentials to gain access to sensitive areas of the network, enabling further lateral movement and data collection.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 - Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: Mapping the network and identifying key assets and processes, preparing for lateral movement and data collection.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Moving across the network to reach SCADA control servers, expanding the scope of the attack within the target infrastructure.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 - Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Collecting operational data to understand system functions, potentially for reverse engineering or sabotage purposes.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277 - Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Sending collected data back to the command and control server, ensuring the attackers maintain a steady stream of valuable information.

stage.name: Impact
stage.technique_name: Disruption of Industrial Control System Operations
stage.description: Altering system parameters to disrupt water treatment processes, potentially leading to environmental harm or safety risks.

Scenario.summary: The attack begins with reconnaissance to gather OSINT on the target's SCADA systems, followed by the development of custom malware tailored to exploit known vulnerabilities. Initial access is achieved through phishing campaigns targeting employees, allowing the attackers to deploy malware and establish persistence. Privilege escalation enables control over critical SCADA processes, while defense evasion techniques ensure the attack remains undetected. Credential access and lateral movement expand the attack's footprint, culminating in data collection, exfiltration, and ultimately, the disruption of industrial control system operations, leading to significant impacts on water treatment processes.
